sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the before/after comparison:
- Charcoal at (51,8)-(52,10) moved to (49,8)-(50,10) - moved up by 2 rows
- Green at (53,8)-(53,10) moved to (51,8)-(51,10) - moved up by 2 rows
- Charcoal at (49,41)-(50,43) stayed at (49,41)-(50,43) - didn't move
- Purple at (51,41)-(51,43) stayed at (51,41)-(51,43) - didn't move
- Charcoal at (27,52)-(28,54) stayed - didn't move
- Yellow at (29,52)-(29,54) stayed - didn't move
- Yellow at (47,48)-(47,49) stayed - didn't move
- Green at (41,14)-(41,15) stayed - didn't move
- Purple at (39,36)-(39,37) stayed - didn't move
So clicking the blue block at (56, 12) only moved the blocks in the leftmost column (cols 8-10). The blue block at (56, 12) is in the leftmost white column (cols 6-13).
Looking at the layout:
- Leftmost column: cols 6-13, blue block at (56, 12)-(57, 13)
- Second column: cols 16-23, blue block at (56, 16)-(57, 17)
- Third column: cols 28-35, blue block at (56, 28)-(57, 29)
- Fourth column: cols 38-47, blue block at (56, 38)-(57, 39)
- Fifth column: cols 50-57, blue block at (56, 50)-(57, 51)
Each column has colored blocks that can be moved up by clicking the blue block at the bottom. The goal is probably to move all colored blocks to the top of their columns.
Looking at the current state:
- Leftmost column (cols 8-10): charcoal at (49,8)-(50,10), green at (51,8)-(51,10) - already moved up
- Second column (cols 14-15): green at (41,14)-(41,15) - already at top?
- Third column (cols 36-37): purple at (39,36)-(39,37) - already at top?
- Fourth column (cols 41-43): charcoal at (49,41)-(50,43), purple at (51,41)-(51,43) - need to move up
- Fifth column (cols 48-54): yellow at (47,48)-(47,49), charcoal at (27,52)-(28,54), yellow at (29,52)-(29,54)
Wait, the fifth column seems to have blocks at different positions. Let me look at the image again:
- The rightmost white column (cols 50-57) has a yellow block at the top and a charcoal block below it.
- The fourth column (cols 38-47) has a purple block at the top.
- The third column (cols 28-35) has nothing visible at the top.
- The second column (cols 16-23) has nothing visible at the top.
- The leftmost column (cols 6-13) has a green block at the top.
Actually, looking more carefully at the image, I see:
- Leftmost column: white base, blue block, green block on top of charcoal
- Second column: white base, blue block
- Third column: white base, blue block
- Fourth column: white base, blue block, purple block on top of charcoal
- Fifth column: white base, blue block, yellow block on top of charcoal
The goal might be to move all colored blocks to the same height, or to stack them in a specific order. Let me try clicking the blue blocks in other columns to see what happens.
Actually, looking at the image more carefully, I see there are black horizontal bars connecting the columns. These might be targets or obstacles. Let me try clicking the blue blocks in all columns to move the colored blocks up.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Clicking blue at (56, 12) moved blocks in leftmost column up by 2 rows.
# Need to click blue blocks in other columns to move their blocks up.
# Blue blocks are at: (56,12), (56,16), (56,24), (56,28), (56,34), (56,38), (56,46), (56,50)

# Let me check which columns have colored blocks that need moving up.
# From the initial state:
# - Col 8-10: charcoal at (51,8)-(52,10), green at (53,8)-(53,10) -> moved to (49,8)-(50,10) and (51,8)-(51,10)
# - Col 41-43: charcoal at (49,41)-(50,43), purple at (51,41)-(51,43) -> didn't move
# - Col 52-54: charcoal at (27,52)-(28,54), yellow at (29,52)-(29,54) -> didn't move
# - Col 48-49: yellow at (47,48)-(47,49) -> didn't move
# - Col 14-15: green at (41,14)-(41,15) -> didn't move
# - Col 36-37: purple at (39,36)-(39,37) -> didn't move

# Let me try clicking the blue block at (56, 38) for the fourth column
print("Trying click on blue block at (56, 38):")
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 56, 'col': 38}], intent='solve')
print(f"  level_completed={last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ['c', 'N', 'p', 'Y'] and n['pixels'] <= 6:
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  {n['color']} at ({min(rows)},{min(cols)})-({max(rows)},{max(cols)})")

print(f"Current state: {state_graph.current}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
